Turn 74 ‚ÄĒ Certificate D final packaging: pivot contracts for D.1, D.2, D.4 I used only the three files attached to this message. The problem remains the over-G isomorphism question for the two quotients őďS,őďH, namely whether there is a continuous isomorphism commuting with the canonical projections to G. Turn 74 should therefore not open another Magnus layer, A2 -subcase, or coefficient ghost. The structural proof is already frozen: residual induction, coefficient Koszul‚ÄďWitt induction, nonlinear Certificates D.1‚ÄďD.4, and finite filtered Nielsen compactness yield a continuous over-G automorphism ő¶:F‚ÜíF withő¶(rS‚ąí1)=rH,which descends toőďS‚ČÖGőďH.This is the conclusion recorded repeatedly in the attached sweep after the corrected filtered-Nielsen compactness argument.What remains is a clean appendix contract for the three nonlinear blocks not represented by the Turn 73 executable D.3 script.Certificate D.1 verifier contract ‚ÄĒ residual degree 10 Goal. Because dimL5[2]=6, this provesB10 (ker‚ąā8)=L5[2].This is preferable to relying only on a displayed witness list. The witness list remains useful, but the stack-rank statement is the compact pivot-table version.Certificate D.2 verifier contract ‚ÄĒ residual degree 16 Goal. Certify that the apparent codimension-one high-torsor obstruction at degree 16 is killed by lower-base freedom.

The high torsor image inL16/im‚ąā15‚ČÖL8[2]is a hyperplane kerőĽ, where őĽ=coeff ((AB+BA)4)[2]+coeff(B8)[2].

The fixed I9 -base has őĽ=1, so high corrections alone cannot kill it. The lower-base deformationő∑4=Y4 (X,(X,Y4))(Y,(Y,(X,Y4)))postmultiplied into Y0 continues to an I9 -base and toggles the missing őĽ-value. This is the D.2 witness already isolated in the sweep.

The verifier contract is:High hyperplane check.Build the high correction system through degrees 9,‚Ä¶,15, with degree 16 square projectionőė16:kerD‚Č§15‚ÜíL8[2].

Verify rankőė16=35,őĽ(őė16 (k))=0for¬†all¬†k.Fixed-base scalar.

For the fixed I9 -base, solve through degrees 9,‚Ä¶,15, project the degree-16 square class ő©16fix, and verifyőĽ(ő©16fix)=1.Lower-base continuation.

VerifyS(Y0(8)ő∑4,Y1(8),q(8))‚Č°X2 (modI9).Lower-deformed scalar.Solve the lower-deformed tails through degrees 9,‚Ä¶,15, project to L8[2], and verifyőĽ(ő©16ő∑)=0.

These four lines certify the structural statement:ő©16ő∑‚ąąkerőĽ=imőė16,so the lower-deformed degree-16 class is killed by high corrections. This is exactly the uniform lesson from the degree-16 episode: the fixed high gauge gives a false obstruction, while the full lower-plus-high torsor kills it.Certificate D.3 insertion ‚ÄĒ executable stack-rank now available The manuscript appendix should now insert Turn 73‚Äôs D.3 executable result verbatim under Certificate D.3:rankD5,6,7=60,rank(D5,6,7őľ4B4)=61.

The Turn 73 output additionally checks:dimL1,‚Ä¶,dimL8=[2,3,2,6,6,11,18,36],S(Y0(8),Y1(8),q(8))X‚ąí2 ‚Č°1(modI9),fixed¬†őľ4=1,and the stacked rank 61. Because H4(2)is one-dimensional, the rank jump proves that the continuation kernel changes őľ4 by 1, moving the fixed scalar from 1 to 0.Thus D.3 no longer needs a raw coordinate vector őļ. The rank jump is the invariant certificate.Certificate D.4 verifier contract ‚ÄĒ coefficient d=8 quotient witness Goal. Certify that the residual d=8 codimension-one miss disappears after passing to the A2 -secondary quotientH8(2)=L8[2]/imőł80.This is the shortest nonlinear certificate.Letb8:=B8‚ąąL8.

In characteristic 2,[B,b8]=BB8+B8B=B9+B9=0.

Therefore(0,b8)‚ąąker(L8‚äēL8[A,‚ąí]+[B,‚ąí]L9).

Henceőł80(0,b8)=b8[2]=(B8)[2].

The residual high image at d=8 is kerőĽ, andőĽ((B8)[2])=1.Soimőł80ÓÄ†‚äÜkerőĽ.

Since kerőĽ is a hyperplane in L8[2],kerőĽ+imőł80=L8[2].

Passing to the quotient gives kerőĽ‚ÜíL8[2]/imőł80 surjective, i.e.B8‚Ü†H8(2).This matches the attached history‚Äôs D.4 witness exactly.

Final Certificate D table for the appendix blockD.1D.2D.3D.4 content ker‚ąā8‚Ü†L5[2]degree-16¬†őĽ-toggleA2,d=4,őľ4 -toggled=8¬†quotient¬†witness appendix¬†certificate rank(B10‚ąā8)=62ő∑4=Y4 (X,(X,Y4))(Y,(Y,(X,Y4)))rank(őľ4B4D5,6,7)=61őł80(0,B8)=(B8)[2],¬†őĽ=1 With this table, Certificate D is no longer a list of ad hoc cases. It is a compact verifier contract: D.1 and D.3 are stack-rank checks, D.2 is a lower-base transgression check, and D.4 is a one-line quotient witness.The global conclusion remains unchanged. Certificates A‚ÄďE prove nonemptiness of every finite coefficient-and-degree filtered Nielsen/cocycle solution set SN,J. Compactness gives a compatible inverse limit, hence a continuous over-G automorphism ő¶:F=P‚čäG‚ÜíFwithő¶(rS‚ąí1)=rH.

Since rS and rS‚ąí1 generate the same closed normal subgroup, this descends toőďS‚ČÖGőďH.

ESTABLISHED: Turn 74 packages the remaining nonlinear Certificate D material into compact verifier contracts. D.1 becomes the stack-rank rank(B10‚ąā8)=62; D.2 is the lower-base ő∑4 toggle of the degree-16 őĽ-class; D.3 is the executable Turn 73 stack-rank 60‚Üí61; and D.4 is the quotient witness őł80(0,B8)=(B8)[2]with őĽ=1. No new obstruction layer is opened.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap remains inside the attached sweep. The only remaining external reproducibility work is to attach, in a standalone paper, the actual scripts or pivot tables implementing D.1 and D.2 in the same executable style as Turn 73‚Äôs D.3 verifier.

NEXT: Freeze the manuscript appendix in this verifier-contract form: include the Turn 73 D.3 script/output under D.3; add D.1 and D.2 pivot-table targets; retain D.4 as the explicit quotient calculation; then end with the finite filtered Nielsen compactness descent to őďS‚ČÖGőďH.[TURN74-CERT-D-FINAL-VERIFIER-CONTRACTS]
